title: Bat Removal & Exclusion Services in Grand Concourse, Bronx
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Grand Concourse, Bronx. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Bats roosting in your Grand Concourse property require immediate professional attention to protect your health and building integrity. Our expert bat removal and exclusion services safely eliminate bat colonies from attics, chimneys, and wall voids throughout the Grand Concourse area. We provide humane removal, thorough exclusion, and comprehensive cleanup to ensure bats don't return to your Bronx home or business.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has over 15 years of experience handling bat infestations in Grand Concourse's historic buildings and residential properties. Our licensed technicians use proven exclusion methods that comply with New York wildlife protection laws while permanently solving your bat problem.
localContext: Grand Concourse's beautiful Art Deco apartment buildings and older residential structures often provide ideal roosting sites for bats in attics, eaves, and architectural details. From the historic Joyce Kilmer Park area to properties near Yankee Stadium, we've helped countless Grand Concourse residents reclaim their homes from bat colonies.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Grand Concourse?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Grand Concourse typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New York City?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bats are protected wildlife in New York, so removal must be done humanely and during appropriate seasons. We follow all NYC and state regulations, using exclusion methods that allow bats to leave but not re-enter, typically performed outside of maternity season (May-August) when flightless young may be present.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Grand Concourse building?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around small openings, and seeing bats enter or exit your building at sunset. Bat guano has a distinct musty odor and can accumulate quickly beneath roosting sites.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection to locate all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that let bats exit but not return, sealing all potential entry points (gaps as small as 3/8 inch), attic cleanup and sanitation to remove guano and odors, and a warranty to ensure bats don't return to your property.
